channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Slot's Isak gamble backfires as Liverpool trophy hopes end
item.description: Arne Slot's gamble to start Alexander Isak against PSG backfires with his own future also in the balance after a Champions League exit.

item.title: Success over Spain - but how can England get best out of classy James?
item.description: How can Sarina Wiegman get the best from one of England's most talented footballers, Lauren James?

item.title: Slot fears Ekitike injury is 'really bad'
item.description: Liverpool boss Arne Slot says Hugo Ekitike's injury 'looks really bad' after the France striker was taken off on a stretcher during Tuesday's Champions League loss to Paris St-Germain.

item.title: Shelvey to quit playing and manage UAE minnows
item.description: Former England midfielder Jonjo Shelvey retires from playing and takes over as manager of UAE third tier side Arabian Falcons FC.

item.title: McArdle praises NI's 'intent' in big win over Malta
item.description: Northern Ireland boss Michael McArdle praises the "intent" his side showed in his first game in charge as they eased to a 4-0 win over Malta in World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Will Iraola's summer exit speed up managerial merry-go-round?
item.description: Andoni Iraola's decision to leave Bournemouth could make it a frantic summer in the Premier League's managerial market, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: Wales must take care of own business - Wilkinson
item.description: Rhian Wilkinson says Wales must "take care of our own business" before focusing on what looks set to be a key Women's World Cup qualifier against Czech Republic in June.

item.title: Man Utd watch Cherries pair - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United watch Premier League pair, Bayern Munich want Harry Kane's England team-mate, plus who is next for the Bournemouth job.

item.title: 'No fear, pure fire' - Arteta defiant on pivotal week
item.description: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta wants the club to play with "pure fire" as they prepare for one of their biggest weeks in years.

item.title: Terry attends Colchester game amid takeover reports
item.description: Former England defender John Terry attends Colchester United's win over Accrington amid reports that he is part of a consortium looking to buy the club.

item.title: 'I wasn't surprised' - Slot critical of overturned Liverpool penalty
item.description: Liverpool boss Arne Slot believes the penalty that was overturned by VAR in his side's Champions League second-leg defeat by PSG would "usually" have been given, but says the Reds have had bad luck with refereeing decisions this season.

item.title: 'It's make or break for him' - is it silverware or the sack for Arteta?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League striker and BBC Sport pundit Chris Sutton says Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta must win the Premier League or the Champions League this season or risk losing his job at the Emirates.

item.title: Okafor fires Leeds to first league win at Old Trafford since 1981
item.description: Leeds boost their Premier League survival hopes with a 2-1 win against 10-man Manchester United at Old Trafford.

item.title: Martinez red card 'shocking decision' - Carrick
item.description: Manchester United boss Michael Carrick says Lisandro Martinez's red card for pulling Dominic Calvert-Lewin's hair was a "shocking decision" during a 2-1 defeat by Leeds United.

item.title: Coaching or confidence - What should De Zerbi prioritise at Spurs?
item.description: Speaking on the Monday Night Club, former Premier League defender Nedum Onuoha says new Tottenham head coach Roberto de Zerbi is right to focus on raising morale rather than tactics as he looks to keep the club in the Premier League.

item.title: Should hair pulling be violent conduct?
item.description: Manchester United's Lisandro Martinez faces a three-match ban for pulling Dominic Calvert-Lewin's hair. Is the punishment fair?

item.title: Man City's Semenyo highlights further racial abuse
item.description: Manchester City forward Antoine Semenyo has highlighted further racial abuse he has received this season.

item.title: Walsh hits century - but who has most England Women caps?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has made the most appearances for the England Women's football team after Keira Walsh reached a century against Spain.

item.title: Three points off top, so why is Sutton ruling Celtic out?
item.description: BBC Scotland dissects the former Celtic striker's reasons and examines the substance behind them.

item.title: 'It's a real issue' - who is the back-up to Lucy Bronze?
item.description: Alex Scott, Fara Williams, Ellen White and Steph Houghton discuss the challenge England face in finding a back-up and eventual successor to Lucy Bronze.

item.title: Man City v Arsenal - winner takes all?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ben Foster and Pat Nevin discuss how the Premier League showdown between Man City and Arsenal could pan out - and what it will mean for the title race.

item.title: Farke 'proud' of 'brave' Leeds
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says they were "brave" as they beat Manchester United 2-1 for their first league win at Old Trafford since 1981.

item.title: How relentless Manchester City punished Chelsea
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ben Foster and Pat Nevin praise Manchester City's second-half performance as they overpower Chelsea to secure a 3-0 away win at Stamford Bridge.

item.title: Tottenham players don't need a coach - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham manager Roberto De Zerbi says he needs to be a "big brother" and "father" figure to his players after their 1-0 defeat at Sunderland in his first game in charge leaves his side in the bottom three of the Premier League.

item.title: Irish deserve praise for victory in Poland - Ward
item.description: Republic of Ireland manager Carla Ward says she hopes her players "get the praise they deserve" for their 3-2 win over Poland in Gdansk.

item.title: Sexist abuse of Union Berlin boss 'embarrassing'
item.description: Union Berlin director of football Horst Heldt condemns "insane" sexist abuse directed at new head coach Marie-Louise Eta.

item.title: Mascherano leaves Inter Miami role
item.description: Former Liverpool and Barcelona midfielder Javier Mascherano steps down as manager of MLS side Inter Miami.

item.title: Neville's Overlap buys Goldbridge's YouTube channels
item.description: Gary Neville's The Overlap buys Manchester United influencer Mark Goldbridge's YouTube channels.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Falkirk boss McGlynn eyes pinnacle of cup final
item.description: Manager John McGlynn says leading Falkirk out at a Scottish Cup final would represent the "pinnacle" of his career as the club prepare for a crucial semi-final against Dunfermline Athletic this weekend.

item.title: 'You don't want an opportunity just because you're female'
item.description: Alex Scott, Fara Williams, Ellen White and Steph Houghton debate Fifa's new ruling that every team in international women's football tournaments must have at least one female head coach or assistant coach.

item.title: De Zerbi has a lot of work to do with Spurs - Nevin
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Pat Nevin looks at how Tottenham's new head coach Roberto de Zerbi set up his side against Sunderland, and the issues that cost Spurs in their 1-0 defeat at the Stadium of Light.

item.title: Who will finish 18th in the Premier League this season?
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Ben Foster and Pat Nevin have their say on which team they think are most likely to finish the season in 18th spot and be relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: Palace kept believing in Newcastle win - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says he wants to keep his team's forward momentum going after Jean-Philippe Mateta came off the bench to score a late double in their 2-1 win over Newcastle United in the Premier League.

item.title: Newcastle defeat to Palace 'deflating' - Howe
item.description: Newcastle United manager Eddie Howe says his sides 2-1 defeat by Crystal Palace was "deflating" and calls the decision to award a late penalty against them "soft", as The Magpies fall to 14th in the Premier League.

item.title: Villa are 'motivated and excited' by Champions League prospect - Emery
item.description: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery says his side are feeling "motivated and excited" at the prospect of qualifying for the Champions League next season, after their 1-1 draw away to Nottingham Forest in the Premier League.

item.title: Chelsea need to improve their resilience - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior admits his side need to "improve their resilience in difficult moments" and focus on winning their next league game against Manchester United to stay in contention for a Champions League spot.

item.title: Birmingham's Ducksch charged with drink driving
item.description: Birmingham City striker Marvin Ducksch is charged with drink driving following a three-vehicle collision on Easter Monday.

item.title: Football refs need cardiac training, says coroner
item.description: A coroner has urged the Football Association to better equip people with cardiac awareness.

item.title: Is this Leicester's last chance to avoid relegation?
item.description: Ten years after winning the Premier League, Leicester are on the verge of relegation to League One.

item.title: When could Wolves be relegated?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how soon Wolves can officially be relegated from the Premier League.

item.title: Carrick says Martinez hair-pull red card 'one of worst decisions I've seen'
item.description: Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez is sent off after 56 minutes for pulling the hair of Leeds United striker Dominic Calvert-Lewin at Old Trafford.

item.title: Retrial over death of Argentina legend Maradona begins
item.description: The first trial - which saw members of Maradona's medical team accused of poor care - collapsed after a judge let cameras into the court.

item.title: Wales to face Romania in summer friendly
item.description: Craig Bellamy's Wales are to face Romania in a friendly in Bucharest in June after both missed out on World Cup qualification.

item.title: Ranking the biggest Premier League title slip-ups
item.description: With Arsenal desperate to banish their title-bottling demons, we look at the top 10 teams who have let Premier League leads slip.

item.title: A monumental night for Leeds - what does it mean in survival scrap?
item.description: A monumental victory over Manchester United at Old Trafford has given Leeds United a priceless lifeline in their quest for Premier League survival.

item.title: What is going on with Garnacho?
item.description: Alejandro Garnacho is struggling to stand out at Chelsea - so can the £40m signing from Manchester United still realise his potential at Stamford Bridge?

item.title: Man City smell blood - why Arsenal should fear title rivals
item.description: Manchester City capitalise on Arsenal's latest slip-up in style - and now Pep Guardiola's side must be feared as the Premier League title race hots up.

item.title: 54 years, 48 leagues, 2,000 grounds - the ultimate football odyssey
item.description: BBC Sport travels with the UK's ultimate groundhopper to the final match of a decades-long, history-making journey.

item.title: Will Romero's tears become the enduring image of Tottenham's season?
item.description: Cristian Romero's tears at Sunderland could prove the lasting image of Tottenham's season if it ends with relegation, writes Phil McNulty.

item.title: All February and March's done deals, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who is one card away from suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: When can Arsenal win the Premier League?
item.description: Arsenal are six points clear at the top of the Premier League, but when can they officially win the title?

item.title: 'I wasn't mature enough for Scotland, now I can make my gran proud'
item.description: Miri Taylor "wasn't in the right headspace" to play for Scotland until she moved to Aston Villa. Now, she's striving for next summer's World Cup.

item.title: Falkirk will 'learn lessons' after alleged Rangers fan incidents
item.description: Falkirk condemn alleged fan incidents and sectarian chanting in Sunday's 6-3 Scottish Premiership defeat by Rangers.

item.title: Rangers to host meeting over refereeing concerns - gossip
item.description: Rangers are reportedly set to host a meeting over refereeing standards in Scotland as a Celtic legend rules his former club out of the title race despite their weekend win.

item.title: Tottenham in advanced talks to sign Robertson
item.description: Tottenham are in advanced talks over a free transfer for Liverpool defender Andy Robertson this summer.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: How fluid PSG exposed Slot's back-five tweak
item.description: Arne Slot changed Liverpool's shape for their Champions League quarter-final first leg - but their tactics were still bested by European champions Paris St-Germain. Umir Irfan explains how.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
